Core Modules#

class bam.actuator.Actuator(testbench_class: Testbench)[source]#

Bases: object

set_model(model)[source]#
reset()[source]#
load_log(log: dict)[source]#

Called when a log is loaded, can be used to retrieve specific values (eg: input voltage, kp gain, etc.))

initialize()[source]#
control_unit() → str[source]#

Return the unit of the control signal (eg: “volts”, “amps”)

compute_control(q_target: float, q: float, dq: float, dt: float) → float | None[source]#

The control (e.g volts or amps) produced by the actuator, given the position error and current configruation

compute_torque(control: float | None, torque_enable: bool, q: float, dq: float) → float[source]#

The torque [Nm] produced by the actuator, given the control signal and current configuration

get_extra_inertia() → float[source]#

Get apparent inertia

to_mujoco()[source]#
class bam.actuator.VoltageControlledActuator(testbench_class: Testbench, vin: float, kp: float, error_gain: float, max_pwm: float = 1.0)[source]#

Bases: Actuator

load_log(log: dict)[source]#

Called when a log is loaded, can be used to retrieve specific values (eg: input voltage, kp gain, etc.))

initialize()[source]#

Default initialization for kt and R that should be set in a voltage controlled servo

control_unit() → str[source]#

Return the unit of the control signal (eg: “volts”, “amps”)

compute_control(q_target: float, q: float, dq: float, dt: float) → float | None[source]#

Assumes the motor is using a kp controller This can be overloaded if more custom behaviour is used

compute_torque(control: float | None, torque_enable: bool, q: float, dq: float) → float[source]#

Computes the torque from DC motor equation

to_mujoco()[source]#
class bam.model.Model(load_dependent: bool = False, directional: bool = False, stribeck: bool = False, quadratic: bool = False, name: str = None, title: str = '')[source]#
reset() → None[source]#

Resets the model internal state

set_actuator(actuator: Actuator) → None[source]#
compute_frictions(motor_torque: float, external_torque: float, dtheta: float) → tuple[source]#
get_parameters() → dict[source]#

This returns the list of parameters that can be optimized.

get_parameter_values() → dict[source]#

Return a dict containing parameter values

load_parameters(json_file: str) → list[source]#

Load parameters from a given filename

load_parameters_from_dict(data: dict) → list[source]#

Load parameters from a given dict

class bam.model.DummyModel[source]#
bam.model.load_model(json_file: str)[source]#
bam.model.load_model_from_dict(data: dict)[source]#
class bam.parameter.Parameter(value: float, min: float, max: float, optimize: bool = True)[source]#
class bam.testbench.Testbench[source]#

A Testbench is a physical setup used to test the motors

The equation of motion of a Test Bench is given by: tau = M(q) * ddq + C(q, dq)

where: - tau is the torque applied to the motor - M(q) is the mass matrix - ddq is the acceleration - C(q, dq) is the bias force

compute_mass(q: float) → float[source]#

Compute the mass

compute_bias(q: float, dq: float) → float[source]#

Compute the bias force

class bam.testbench.Pendulum(log: dict)[source]#
compute_mass(q: float, dq: float) → float[source]#

In the case of a pendulum, the mass is an inertia

compute_bias(q: float, dq: float) → float[source]#
class bam.simulate.Simulator(model: Model)[source]#
reset(q: float = 0.0, dq: float = 0.0)[source]#

Resets the simulation to a given state

step(control: None | float, torque_enable: bool, dt: float)[source]#

Steps the simulation for dt given the applied control

rollout_log(log: dict, reset_period: float = None, simulate_control: bool = False)[source]#

Read a given log dict and return the sequential reached positions

class bam.logs.Logs(directory: str)[source]#
split(selector_kp: int) → Logs[source]#

Extract ratio% of the logs from the current object to

make_batch() → dict[source]#

Make a batch log from all the logs. In a batch log, all entries are vectorized. For example, batch[“mass”] is a vector of all masses batch[“entries”][0][“position”] will be a vector of all positions

bam.trajectory.cubic_interpolate(keyframes: list, t: float)[source]#

duration = None#

duration = 6.0#

duration = 6.0#

duration = 6.0#

duration = 6.0#
class bam.trajectory.Nothing[source]#
duration = 6.0#

bam.message.bright(message)[source]#
bam.message.run_or_fail(cmd)[source]#
bam.message.emphasis(text)[source]#
bam.message.yellow(text)[source]#
bam.message.red(text)[source]#
bam.message.success(text)[source]#
bam.message.die(message)[source]#
bam.message.print_parameter(name, value, infos=None, warning=None)[source]#
class bam.mujoco.MujocoController(model: Model, actuator: str, mujoco_model: mujoco.MjModel, mujoco_data: mujoco.MjData)[source]#

A MujocoController is a class allowing to apply the torque and update frictions from the computed model during a simulation.

Parameters:

  • model (Model) – model to use (can be loaded using load_model)

  • actuator (str) – actuator to control Note1: the actuated joint properties will be updated Note2: this can be a list of actuators

  • mujoco_model (mujoco.MjModel) – the mujoco model

  • mujoco_data (mujoco.MjData) – the mujoco data

get_q_target(name: str) → float[source]#
set_q_target(name: str, q_target: float)[source]#
reset(qpos)[source]#
update()[source]#

Update the controlled actuator(s) data: - Torque to apply - Friction parameters - Damping

bam.mujoco.load_config(path: str, mujoco_model: mujoco.MjModel, mujoco_data: mujoco.MjData, kp: float, vin: float) → tuple[source]#

Loads a BAM configuration file and returns the list of controllers and the mapping dicts.

Parameters:

  • path (str) – path to the configuration file

  • mujoco_model (mujoco.MjModel) – the mujoco model

  • mujoco_data (mujoco.MjData) – the mujoco data

  • kp (float) – the proportional gain

  • vin (float) – the input voltage

Returns:

list of controllers, dofs to model mapping, dofs to id mapping

Return type:

list
